source_jobs.title: Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
About Foursquare


Foursquare is the leading independent location technology and data cloud platform, helping businesses connect the digital and physical worlds with precision and purpose. Our products power everything from market analytics to targeted experiences, relied on by some of the world’s most iconic brands.



But what makes Foursquare truly special is how we work. We're a team of builders, problem-solvers, and collaborators who care deeply about impact – both in the products we ship and the way we show up for each other. Great outcomes come from strong collaboration and shared ownership, and we believe growth happens through real challenges – whether that’s leveling up a skill, tackling unfamiliar problems, or pushing a project forward in a new way. If you're excited by cutting-edge tech and a culture that values both autonomy and teamwork, you’ll thrive here.



About the Position

Foursquare is looking for an Account Executive, New Business to join our team in our New York City office; on a hybrid schedule of Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday in office.

Reporting to our VP of Sales, you’ll be responsible for identifying and closing net-new business opportunities across Foursquare’s full product portfolio; including media, measurement, and data solutions.

The New Business Sales team is the engine driving Foursquare’s next phase of growth. As an Account Executive, you’ll prospect, pitch, and close new deals across agencies, brands, and strategic partners. Success in this role requires a self-starter mindset, a results-oriented approach, and a high level of integrity. You’ll play a key role in expanding our customer base and unlocking new revenue streams.

In this role, you’ll

 - Build expertise and business around media measurement and targeting solutions

 - Effectively and consistently develop pipeline and convert new accounts 

 - Secure attention and investment from Marketers, their agencies and leaders across Measurement, Programmatic and Investment  

 - Leverage internal FSQ resources to maximize win rates 

 - Create and run point on new business outreach and liaison with Account Mgmt on established logos running

 - Work with cross functional team of Account Managers and Customer Success to win, nurture and build external overall client relationships

 - Forecast accurately, using SFDC (CRM system)

 - Adhere to ethical standards of personal conduct and business rules, in line with Foursquare’s leadership principles, when making decisions or executing tasks

What you’ll need

 - Proven track record of success in closing new logos

 - AE role for at least 3 years +

 - Experience selling in to agencies and brands direct

 - Good marketplace relationships with relevant and potential customers

 - Strong communication and influencing skills; both written and verbal

 - Ability to drive results; track record of high performance and default to action

 - Alignment and demonstration of our Foursquare Leadership principles

 - Professional philosophy that is driven thru honesty and integrity

 - Your own unique talents! If you don’t meet 100% of the qualifications outlined above, we encourage and welcome you to still apply!

At Foursquare, we’re committed to offering competitive pay and benefits that reflect market standards and the unique strengths each candidate brings. Actual compensation is based on a variety of factors, including skills, experience, and office location. We also regularly review our compensation practices to ensure fairness, equity, and alignment with evolving market trends.

While final offers for this role may vary slightly based on individual qualifications and experience, we aim to stay within the published range below to support pay equity and transparency across the organization.

Compensation Package Details:

 - Total estimated cash compensation: $175,000 – $240,000, which includes the following components:
   
   - Annual base salary range: $100,000– $140,000
   
   - Annual sales-based

source_jobs.title: Data Operations Associate (Fixed-term)
source_jobs.description:
About Foursquare


Foursquare is the leading independent location technology and data cloud platform, helping businesses connect the digital and physical worlds with precision and purpose. Our products power everything from market analytics to targeted experiences, relied on by some of the world’s most iconic brands.



But what makes Foursquare truly special is how we work. We're a team of builders, problem-solvers, and collaborators who care deeply about impact – both in the products we ship and the way we show up for each other. Great outcomes come from strong collaboration and shared ownership, and we believe growth happens through real challenges – whether that’s leveling up a skill, tackling unfamiliar problems, or pushing a project forward in a new way. If you're excited by cutting-edge tech and a culture that values both autonomy and teamwork, you’ll thrive here.



About the Position

Foursquare is looking for a Data Operations Associate to join our team in our Belgrade office; on a hybrid schedule of Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday in office.



We are looking for a highly motivated Data Operations Associate to join our team. In this role, you will be primarily focused on executing web crawls, manipulating data, and utilizing internal pipeline tools to ensure data quality and availability. This is an internally focused, highly technical role where persistence, vigilance, and the ability to learn and iterate quickly are essential. If you enjoy solving technical puzzles, building and maintaining data flows, and continuously learning new skills, we want you on our team!

Please note that this is a fixed-term position for an initial period of one year, with the possibility of extension.



In this role, you’ll

 - Web Crawling & Extraction: Execute and manage web crawling initiatives, extracting data, and adapting quickly when target site structures change.

 - Data Manipulation & Pipelines: Utilize internal tools to perform data cleanup, matching, and transformations to ensure high data quality and reliability.

 - Technical Troubleshooting: Be vigilant in monitoring data pipelines; investigate issues, read and interpret log files, implement workarounds, and escalate complex technical problems.

 - Continuous Improvement: Learn and adapt to new internal tools and technical processes rapidly, iterating on existing workflows to improve efficiency.

 - Documentation: Create and maintain technical process documentation and how-to guides to empower internal teams
   

What you’ll need

 - Proficient level of written and verbal English

 - 1+ years of experience working in data-oriented customer operations, technical support, analytics, or similar roles.

 - Solid understanding of Python or Scala, with strong SQL skills.

 - Familiarity with Regular Expressions (Regex) for pattern matching, data extraction, validation, and web content processing.

 - Familiarity with data analysis tools and libraries (e.g., PySpark, pandas), web crawling concepts and execution.

 - General technical troubleshooting skills: ability to read/interpret/transform log files, comfort navigating command-line interfaces (CLI) and APIs, and a strong capacity for independent research.

 - Your own unique talents! If you don’t meet 100% of the qualifications outlined above, we encourage and welcome you to still apply!
   
   

Benefits and Perks

 - 25 days of paid vacation – take the time you need to rest and recharge

 - Private medical insurance – comprehensive coverage for you and your family

 - FitPass – flexible access to gyms, wellness classes, and sports facilities across Serbia

 - Hybrid work schedule – in-person collaboration Tuesday–Thursday, with company-paid lunch on office days

 - Growth Investment Program – funding and resources to support your personal and professional development

 - Tech setup – we’ll provide a company laptop and the essentials to help you get up and running smoothly

#LI-HYBRID
#LI-MM1

source_jobs.title: Customer Analytics Lead
source_jobs.description:
About Foursquare


Foursquare is the leading independent location technology and data cloud platform, helping businesses connect the digital and physical worlds with precision and purpose. Our products power everything from market analytics to targeted experiences, relied on by some of the world’s most iconic brands.



But what makes Foursquare truly special is how we work. We're a team of builders, problem-solvers, and collaborators who care deeply about impact – both in the products we ship and the way we show up for each other. Great outcomes come from strong collaboration and shared ownership, and we believe growth happens through real challenges – whether that’s leveling up a skill, tackling unfamiliar problems, or pushing a project forward in a new way. If you're excited by cutting-edge tech and a culture that values both autonomy and teamwork, you’ll thrive here.

About the Position



Foursquare is looking for a Customer Analytics Lead to join our team in our New York office on a hybrid schedule of Tuesday, Wednesday, and Thursday in office.



In this role, you will support the Customer Analytics function by delivering accurate, timely, and actionable analysis for customer-facing teams. Working closely with your manager and GTM stakeholders, you will execute recurring reporting workflows, analyze campaign performance, troubleshoot reporting questions and translate complex data into clear customer-ready insights.



This is a hands-on analytics role focused on execution, quality control, process improvement, and measurement. You will own projects from intake through completion, validate reporting outputs, investigate data quality issues, improve analytics workflows, and help teams understand performance drivers through methodologies such as attribution, incrementality, and lift analysis.



This role is ideal for someone with early analytics experience who is eager to deepen their measurement expertise, strengthen their reporting skills and help build scalable analytics processes.



What You’ll Need

 - 2-4 years of experience in marketing analytics, customer analytics, business intelligence, data analysis, or related fields, preferably within digital media, SaaS, technology, ad tech, or martech environments.

 - Strong SQL skills and comfort working with datasets, as well as proficiency with Excel or Google Sheets.

 - Experience creating clear tables, charts, summaries, and reporting outputs.

 - Foundational understanding of marketing analytics, campaign reporting, attribution, lift studies, incrementality, or media measurement.

 - Strong attention to detail and a commitment to delivering accurate, high-quality work.

 - Experience with BI tools, dash-boarding platforms, Python, PySpark, or automation tools is a plus.

 - Bachelor’s degree in Statistics, Data Science, Mathematics, Economics, Computer Science, Business Analytics, or a related quantitative field preferred.

 - Your own unique talents. If you don’t meet 100% of the qualifications outlined above, we encourage and welcome you to still apply.



At Foursquare, we’re committed to offering competitive pay and benefits that reflect market standards and the unique strengths each candidate brings. Actual compensation is based on a variety of factors, including skills, experience, and office location. We also regularly review our compensation practices to ensure fairness, equity, and alignment with evolving market trends.



While final offers for this role may vary slightly based on individual qualifications and experience, we aim to stay within the published range below to support pay equity and transparency across the organization.



Compensation Package Details:

 - Total estimated cash compensation: $150,000- $170,000, which includes the following components:
   
   - Annual base salary range: $100,000 - $130,000
   
   - Annual sales-based commission (target): $20,000-$50,000

 - Equity: eligibility to receive Restricted Stock Units (RSUs) subject to
